area: Midtown Raleigh / North Hills District, NC
schema_note: Shares the same local_xz coordinate system as buildings.json (origin at 35.8390, -78.6420). Each road has a 'label' block describing how/where to render its street-name signage in the scene, separate from the geometry path.
accuracy_note: Road paths are reasonable approximations of real street layout/curvature, not GIS-surveyed data. Real street names are accurate; exact curve points are estimated.

roads.name: Six Forks Road

lane_markings.notes: 4-lane arterial: double yellow center, one dashed white divider per direction (2 lanes each side), solid white edge lines. Full crosswalk + stop bar at every signaled intersection.

label.notes: Primary spine -- label should appear at every intersection it passes through, plus optionally a painted/decal road name at 1-2 mid-block points.

roads.notes: Main north-south spine through the district. Slight eastward curve as it goes north.

roads.name: Lassiter Mill Road

lane_markings.notes: 2-lane collector: single solid yellow center, no internal lane dividers (one lane per direction), white edge lines. Crosswalk + stop bar at the signaled crossing with Six Forks Rd.

label.notes: Roughly east-west, crosses Six Forks Rd near the retail core -- label both sides of that crossing.

roads.notes: Passes the RH Raleigh showroom and Village Tavern frontage in real life.

roads.name: Park at North Hills Street

lane_markings.notes: 2-lane local road: dashed yellow center (lower speed than collector/arterial), white edge lines, crosswalk + stop bar at its signaled Six Forks Rd crossing.

label.notes: Local access road curving past the office tower district.

roads.notes: Feeds into Six Forks Rd; this is the street several apartment complexes use as their listed address.

lane_markings.notes: 2-lane local road, same marking style as the other local streets in the district.

label.notes: Local connector through the innovation district.

roads.name: Dartmouth Road

lane_markings.notes: Quiet residential-feeling local street, narrow enough that no center line is typical -- just edge lines. Crosswalk at its stop-sign junction with St Albans Dr, but no stop bar paint since it's a minor unsignaled stop.

label.notes: Quiet residential-feeling local street near The Dartmouth North Hills apartments.

roads.notes: Connects the office cluster area to nearby residential midrises.

roads.name: Midtown Park Walk (internal pedestrian-priority loop)

lane_markings.notes: Shared pedestrian-priority lane -- NO vehicle lane markings (no center line, no dashes, no edge stripe). Instead use a paving-pattern change (e.g. lighter pavers or a brick-style texture band) to distinguish it from the road surface. Still mark a crosswalk-style paving strip where it crosses Park at N Hills St, since pedestrians/cars do cross there, but skip the stop-bar paint -- that junction is stop-sign controlled, not a painted vehicle stop line.

label.notes: Not a real official road name -- generic label for the internal walkable loop ringing the park/shops core. Use small wayfinding-style signs, not vehicle street signs.

roads.notes: Closed loop representing the walkable retail core internal streets (lower speed, wider sidewalks, on-street parking). Use a different road material/width than the arterials -- lighter paving, planters along the edge.

intersections.name: Six Forks Rd & Lassiter Mill Rd
intersections.notes: Major intersection -- main signal-controlled crossing in the district.

intersections.name: Six Forks Rd & Park at N Hills St
intersections.notes: Signal at the office tower district entrance.

intersections.name: Lassiter Mill Rd & St Albans Dr
Park at N Hills St & Midtown Park Walk
intersections.notes: Minor junction into the retail core loop -- stop sign matches the lower-speed pedestrian character there.
